LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PURSUANT TO CCP 452, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION OF DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION. THOSE FACTS ALSO SUPPORT THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT RAISES FACTUAL ISSUES AND MANY OF THE FACTS THAT DEFENDANT STATES ARE MISSING ARE PRESUMPTIVELY WITHIN ITS KNOWLEDGE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE CAUSES OF ACTION PLEADING INTENTIONAL DISCRIMINATION SUPPORT THE CLAIM FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

James Robertson, II, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/AJR)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Continued To Apr-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Pursuant to CCP 452, Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to state a cause of action of disability discrimination. Those facts also support the other causes of action. Defendant raises factual issues and many of the facts that Defendant states are missing are presumptively within its knowledge. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. The causes of action pleading intentional discrimination support the claim for exemplary damages. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
